text.title: Agentic Workflow for Generative UI
text.summary: Subagent patterns, prompt references, and gotchas for AI-driven parallel UI variant generation.

section.title: Agentic Pattern
section.p: Claude subagents generate multiple UI variants from a single brief by running parallel generation calls with different style constraints (minimal, warm-approachable, information-dense). The agent produces structured React/HTML code or Figma-paste-ready specifications. Human curates: selects variants, marks off-brand elements, flags accessibility issues. A second agent pass refines the selected variant based on critique notes.

list.item: haiku — high-volume variant generation (10+ options), design token application to boilerplate
sonnet — complex multi-state component generation, critique synthesis, design brief interpretation

section.title: Prompt and Template References
section.reference: Batch generate multiple UI variants via Claude API in parallel
Prompt for generating 3 styled React component variants from a brief
Prompt for auditing generated UI for WCAG, ARIA, and brand violations

antipattern.description: Agent generates code with library APIs that don't exist or are deprecated.
antipattern.reason: LLMs hallucinate library APIs, especially for newer React versions and niche component libraries; always test generated code in a sandbox before accepting.

antipattern.description: Treating WCAG compliance prompts as sufficient for accessible output.
antipattern.reason: Generated code that targets WCAG compliance often has broken focus order and missing live regions; axe-cli audit is mandatory regardless of prompt instructions.

antipattern.description: Using "modern" or "clean" as style constraints without concrete references.
antipattern.reason: Abstract style terms mean different things every run; add specific style references (typography system, component library names, example URLs) for reproducible output.

antipattern.description: Accepting agent-generated complex component trees without checking against design system composition rules.
antipattern.reason: Agents propose component nesting that violates design system composition rules silently; the output looks correct but breaks at the system level.
